buys.title: 2 Stocks the Entire AI Market Depends On !!
buys.reason: ASML is a Dutch company that manufactures lithography systems, the machines that project circuit patterns onto silicon wafers to create semiconductors. Its extreme ultraviolet (EUV) machines are the only tools on Earth capable of printing today's most advanced chip designs, and no other company makes them. TSMC, Samsung, Intel, and every leading edge chip maker buy from ASML because there is no alternative. EUV is how 3 nanometer chips and 2 nanometer chips exist, and ASML's next generation high NA EUV platform is how the nodes beyond that will be printed. As chips become more complex, more EUV exposures are required per chip, and AI is accelerating this litho intensity even faster than any previous technology cycle. The CEO updated 2026 guidance, increasing the expected revenue range to between 36 billion and 40 billion euros while maintaining gross margin expectations of 51 to 53%. ASML is currently under-shipping demand and cannot build EUV machines fast enough to meet customer demand. By 2027, ASML aims to ship at least 80 tools (up from 44 in the prior year), effectively doubling the chip-making capacity it delivers through EUV alone. Additionally, AI-grade DRAM adoption is accelerating—DRAM now accounts for 51% of net sales systems (up from 30% in Q4 2025), representing a durable new dimension to ASML's growth driven by AI accelerators requiring high bandwidth memory. Once memory makers commit to EUV intensive nodes, they keep buying tools for years, making this a sticky revenue stream rather than a one-time event.
buys.cautions: Primary risk is regulatory. ASML is already prohibited from selling its most advanced EUV chips to China, and the rules governing what it can sell and to whom are still evolving. China currently accounts for 20% of ASML's revenue, and any further tightening of restrictions or extensions of controls to additional product lines could trim that contribution further. Management acknowledged this directly, noting that 2026 revenue guidance range is set specifically to accommodate potential outcomes of ongoing discussions around export control. For a company with a hardware monopoly on the world's most critical technology, the risk isn't competition, it's policy, and the regulatory environment remains live and unpredictable.

buys.title: Top 9 Stocks to BUY NOW (High Growth Stocks)
buys.reason: ASML has borderline monopoly on extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography machines required to manufacture latest-generation chips used in AI processing. Company makes high NA EUV tools costing $250 million each that use lasers to etch chips at molecular level—writing with light is the only viable method to produce latest AI chips. Effectively monopolistic positioning means ASML has no real competitors in this critical bottleneck technology. TSMC and other chip manufacturers must purchase these machines regardless of price to stay competitive in AI chip manufacturing. Elon Musk visited ASML to emphasize importance to chip manufacturing future. New next-generation machine priced at $400 million represents monopoly pricing power given customers (TSMC, Samsung, Intel) have multi-trillion dollar market caps. Current business model is working well with company continuing to grow from monopolistic market position.

Company is vulnerable to geopolitical factors including export restrictions or government intervention in critical semiconductor supply chain. Valuation risk exists if semiconductor cycle turns down or if customers delay next-generation chip manufacturing capacity investments.

buys.reason: The CFO Sheila Verma explained that Robinhood is transitioning from a pure trading platform to a financial super app with three distinct growth arcs: (1) becoming the #1 platform for active traders through continuous product innovation, (2) increasing wallet share by offering banking, credit cards, robo-advisory, and other financial products, and (3) building a global financial ecosystem across retail, B2B, and institutional clients. avoids.title: Netflix Stock is Crashing - Is It A Buy Now?
avoids.reason: Pronk concludes Netflix is trading around fair value, not at a discount, despite being down 45% from all-time highs. He argues the stock was very expensive at peaks due to multiple expansion and has now corrected to fair value rather than becoming cheap. Netflix's business is maturing with decelerating growth rates: revenue growth expected to decelerate from 13.5% in Q2 2026 to 11.7% in 2027 and 10.3% in 2028. The streaming market is becoming increasingly competitive with YouTube and Amazon gaining market share—Netflix gained only 0.2% market share year-over-year while YouTube gained 1.7%, suggesting competitors are performing better. Pronk's DCF analysis yields only 13% compounded annual growth versus higher returns from competing stocks. The advertising business, while growing at 100%, remains a small portion of overall revenue. When it comes to the most advanced chips running AI training and inference at scale, TSMC holds something close to an absolute monopoly with its 3-nanometer and 2-nanometer process nodes being the cutting edge of what silicon can do today. AI has become the core of TSMC's business—the company's high-performance computing (AI accelerators, GPUs, and data center chips) increased 20% quarter over quarter to account for 61% of fourth quarter revenue. The company guided full-year 2026 revenue to grow above 30% in US dollar terms. TSMC is executing a coordinated multi-continent expansion of its 3 nanometer node with a new fab in Taiwan, a second Arizona fab, and Japan's second fab being upgraded to 3 nanometer. The CEO stated they are stepping up CapEx investments to increase N3 capacity, with the CFO noting that CapEx in the next three years will be significantly higher than the past three years (which totaled 101 billion), representing a capital investment cycle of a scale semiconductor manufacturing has never experienced. The shift from generative AI to agentic AI is driving a step up in token consumption and computation needs, with AI accelerator revenue now tracking towards a compounded annual growth rate in the high 50s, potentially nearly doubling in under two years.
buys.cautions: Geographic concentration risk—Taiwan is where the overwhelming majority of TSMC's most advanced manufacturing happens, making any geopolitical escalations involving Taiwan an existential risk to the global AI hardware supply chain and the stock. The Q1 2026 call also surfaced a near-term supply chain vulnerability with situations in the Middle East creating uncertainty around specialty chemicals and gases essential to chip fabrication, though management said they don't anticipate near-term disruption but acknowledged it was too early to quantify any potential impact on profitability.
